definition: The total dollars spent during the financial year by the agency.

The total dollars spent on ICT during the financial year by the agency, including ICT Spend by the centralised ICT group and ICT spend by the business units. ICT Spend includes all capital expenses and operating expenses. However, ICT Spend excludes all depreciation.  Excludes spend on physical ICT equipment whose primary role is not ICT-related, e.g. traffic light controllers, medical equipment and industrial/manufacturing equipment.  The actual amount spent should be reported, regardless of the funding source and whether or not the amount spent was included in any budget.

Total dollars spent during the financial year, in the relevant spending category, incurred as part of the ongoing service delivery activities of the agency. Includes both capital and operational dollars spent, other than dollars spent on Projects, as defined below. Excludes all allowances for depreciation.
Total dollars spent during the financial year incurred as part of projects undertaken to enhance the capability of the agency to deliver ICT and business services to internal clients and the public.

All spend on physical ICT equipment, including purchase, lease, maintenance of the hardware by the hardware vendor, repair, peripherals and consumables charges. Excludes spend on physical ICT equipment whose primary role is not ICT-related e.g. traffic light controllers, medical equipment & industrial/manufacturing equipment.  Excludes spend on internal labour, contractors &external service provider infrastructure support services.

All spend on software supplied by software vendor organisations, including licence, maintenance and support charges by the software vendor. Includes spend on business applications, operating systems and utilities, database software, desktop software and other tools.  Excludes spend on internal labour, contractors and external service provider software support services.
Services which enable transmission of voice and data traffic between agency locations and between agency infrastructure and external parties.

term: Services Provided by External Service Provider
definition: All spend for services delivered under commercial contracts with non-Government external organisations. Under an External Service Provider arrangement, the service provider is responsible for the delivery of the services.  Includes services provided by consultants who are engaged for specific short-term projects, e.g. to produce reports.
All spend for services delivered by another Government agency, e.g. Shared Services. Under such an arrangement, the other agency is responsible for the delivery of the services.

Labour spend should be entered as the fully-burdened cost of employing the relevant FTEs, including base salary, payroll tax, compulsory and voluntary superannuation, annual leave loading, long service leave, work cover levy, high duties, allowances, overtime.  If the labour burdening rate used by the agency is a fixed percentage, this percentage should be stated in the Comments field.
Staff employed directly by the agency, including both full time and part time employees, including staff on fixed term contracts that are filling a government position.

External staff who are employed on a contractual basis by the agency and who report directly to the agency for the work performed. Excludes consultants and staff employed by external service provider organisations.  Includes Temporary staff who are procured under a Temporary Staff Placement Services contract to fill a Government position.

Any ICT Spend during the measurement period (the financial year) which is not reported under one of the categories above.  May include training, conferences, travel etc.  For facilities, include spend on floor space, utilities, air conditioning, power provision etc.

The breakdown of total spend on Applications Development.  Development and major enhancements to business applications. Major enhancements are those incurring 20 days or more of effort. The following activities are included; Requirements, Analysis & Design, Build, Test, Deploy, Documentation and Training, Supply Development Tools, Supply Application Software.  Includes the configuration of middleware software from a business perspective by Applications staff as part of development work. Excludes the installation and technical configuration of middleware software.  Includes Applications database administration activities performed as part of development work, e.g. logical database design. Excludes System database administration activities, e.g. physical database creation.

The breakdown of total spend on Applications Maintenance and Support.  Maintenance of the production applications portfolio and any other applications used by the Business Units to conduct the agency’s business including: 
· Corrective Maintenance which means maintenance associated with correcting software errors ("bug fix").
· Adaptive Maintenance which means the maintenance of software associated with keeping the software useable in a changed or changing environment or minor enhancements to business functionality (less than 20 days of effort).
· Perfective Maintenance which means the maintenance of software associated with improving the efficiency and reliability of the Software and to minimise on-going maintenance.
· Preventive Maintenance which means the maintenance of software associated with efforts to prevent software problems from occurring (e.g. the removal of latent defects).
· Includes minor enhancements incurring less than 20 days of effort.  Includes support provided to end users in relation to the applications used to run the business.
· Includes the configuration and administration of middleware software from a business perspective by Applications staff. Excludes the technical support of middleware software.
· Includes Applications database administration activities performed as part of applications maintenance and minor enhancements, e.g. changes to schema definitions. Excludes System database administration activities, e.g. physical database reorganisation.

The total spend on Managed Applications, which are application-specific services provided by an external service provider or another agency, which include all infrastructure and labour, Applications Maintenance and Support, Application Development (if applicable) and production running for an application.  Include managed applications for which an ongoing contract is in place and any other external applications used to conduct agency business and paid for on a per-use basis (Software as a Service).  A breakdown of spend will typically not be available for this category.  Excludes the Government Radio Network, which is included in the GRN section.

Web Services cover provision of public facing web sites through an external service provider or another agency, which includes all infrastructure and labour, Web Maintenance and Support and Web Development (if applicable).  A breakdown of spend will typically not be available for this category.  Excludes internal costs related to maintaining web content.

The breakdown of total spend on Mainframe.  The Mainframe environment includes the provision, management and monitoring of all staff, hardware, software and other resources used in providing Mainframe infrastructure for mainframe data processing.  The Mainframe environment includes all mainframe elements, including:
· Equipment and peripherals for all Mainframe environments
· Software, including system software, Independent Software Vendor (ISV) software, monitoring software, reporting software, database software and software for all mainframe environments and
· Data network connectivity from the mainframe development, test, pre-production/staging, production environments and disaster recovery environment to the wide area network.
Mainframe support is the support of the mainframe infrastructure. Mainframe operations are the processing operations to run all mainframe applications software and systems software, including on-line and batch processing.

The breakdown of total spend on the Server environment.  The server environment spend is broken down by the type of server operating system.  The server environment encompasses all virtual and physical servers, including Application Servers, Database Servers, Infrastructure Servers, Web Servers and Systems Servers.  The server environment includes peripherals directly attached to these servers.  The server environment software includes the system software, middleware, database and virtualisation software.  The server environment support includes technical (systems administration) and operations support including the on-line and batch processing operations for midrange applications, systems operations, start up and shutdown and server monitoring.

The breakdown of total spend on End User Computing.  The End-User Computing Infrastructure encompasses all End-User Equipment and Software used to support end users and deliver ICT services, including:
· Desktops (standard, enhanced, thin client, virtualised, non-standard and standalone)
· Notebooks (standard, enhanced, light, ruggedised and non-standard)
· Tablets
· Monitors (standard, dual and quad displays)
· Printers and multi-function devices (including consumables for managed print devices)
· Related peripherals, including: additional monitors, USB and standalone hard drives, docking stations, mouse, keyboard, laptop accessories, digital cameras, web-cams, projectors and whiteboards, VoIP phones and standalone scanners
· Software including Standard Operating Environment, operating system, productivity packages and other software
· Any Assistive Technology required by staff with special needs
· Any End User Computing Equipment, Software or devices located outside agency locations, such as home based, for which ICT services are provided and
· Any other devices or software that are used to enable End User Computing capability
End-User Computing spend excludes Service Desk costs.

The breakdown of total spend on Storage. Encompasses all equipment and software to provide storage in support of the ICT services and includes: Storage equipment, Storage devices, including SAN backup disk and tape systems, software, including system software, data backup software and any specialised storage management software, and any associated connectivity.

The breakdown of total spend on Data Network infrastructure.  Data Network infrastructure encompasses all equipment, software and network components required to connect agency locations to end users and includes:
· Switches, routers, firewalls and other data network and wireless devices
· Software, including firmware, system software and any specialised network management tools
· Connectivity, including wireless connectivity
· Network connectivity to the agency’s LAN/WAN, including connectivity to Enterprise Services
· Related peripherals; and any other devices or software used to enable data network and wireless capabilities.
Data Network services means all functions necessary to manage, support and administer the Data Network infrastructure.

The breakdown of total spend on Voice Network infrastructure.  Voice infrastructure encompasses all equipment and software required to provide voice services to agency locations and End Users and includes:
· IP telephony devices, fixed handsets and PABXs
· Software, including firmware, system software and any specialised telephony and IP voice management tools
· Connectivity, including voice email connectivity to email
· Related peripherals; and any other devices or Software used to enable telephony capabilities.
Voice Network services means all functions necessary to manage, support and administer the voice infrastructure.

The breakdown of total spend on Facilities.  Hosting facilities, local ICT infrastructure facilities and telecommunications hubs used to house infrastructure for delivery of ICT services. Facilities includes:
· Floor space, cabinet and rack space, power supply (including UPS power supply), heating, cooling and noise level facilities, monitors, cabling, connectivity, interfaces and network connections.
· Providing and managing secure access to hosting facilities and site based computing rooms.

All equipment and software used to provide Mobile Services: Mobile Services include mobile phones, satellite phone handsets and smartphones with data plans (including iPhone, Samsung Galaxy, etc.)  Excludes mobile carriage costs.

All carriage costs, including Voice, Data and Internet Service Provider (ISP) traffic.  Includes calls made from fixed phone lines, satellite phones, mobile phones (voice, SMS and data), incoming 13/1300 and 18/1800 services.  Includes expenditure on internal and external data links within the agency, and Internet carriage costs provided by external vendors (invoiced per Megabyte download).

The breakdown of total spend on the Government Radio Network.  The Government Radio Network (GRN) involves the provision of radio communications services to multiple entities to provide the following facilities:
· Voice radio network providing radio communications coverage to portable and mobile radio equipment within the network coverage area.
· Mobile data radio network providing a mobile data bearer network to enable communication between vehicle mounted computer equipment and agency applications.
· Alphanumeric paging services to entities within the network coverage area. This network is used by multiple entities for administrative and emergency paging and alerting.

The breakdown of total spend on Service Management including:
· Service Desk Services that provide a point of contact for the recording, tracking and co-ordination of incidents and service requests.   The Service Desk Services include level 1 support for incidents.  The Service Desk Services may also include level 2 support
· Service Integration Services that provide end-to-end service management including the integration of multiple service providers
· Project Management Office (PMO) services. The PMO defines the processes and standards for ICT project and program management within the agency. The PMO monitors and reports on the performance of projects throughout their lifecycles.

The breakdown of total spend on ICT Management. Includes: ICT strategy, application/technology architecture, ICT governance, ICT vendor management, financial management and reporting and ICT executive support.
